remember: Students will be able to recall the concept of consumer equilibrium.
Students will be able to list the conditions for consumer equilibrium.
Students will be able to identify the determinants of consumer demand.
Students will be able to recall the law of demand and its implications.
Students will be able to explain the indifference curve analysis of consumer behaviour.
understand: Students will be able to explain the concept of consumer equilibrium in detail.
Students will be able to differentiate between the conditions for consumer equilibrium and the determinants of consumer demand.
Students will be able to understand the law of demand and its graphical representation.
Students will be able to explain the indifference curve analysis of consumer behaviour, including the concept of marginal rate of substitution.
Students will be able to identify the assumptions of consumer equilibrium and their implications.
apply: Students will be able to apply the concept of consumer equilibrium to real-life scenarios.
Students will be able to use indifference curve analysis to determine consumer equilibrium.
Students will be able to calculate the marginal rate of substitution and its implications on consumer behaviour.
Students will be able to determine the effect of changes in consumer preferences on demand and supply.
Students will be able to analyse the impact of income and price changes on consumer equilibrium.
analyze: Students will be able to critically evaluate the assumptions of consumer equilibrium and their implications.
Students will be able to compare and contrast different theories of consumer behaviour, including indifference curve analysis and budget line analysis.
Students will be able to identify the limitations of consumer equilibrium and suggest areas for further research.
Students will be able to analyse the impact of policy changes on consumer behaviour and demand.
Students will be able to evaluate the role of consumer equilibrium in determining market equilibrium and prices.

ncert_objectives: Explain the concept of consumer equilibrium, its conditions and determinants.
Discuss the law of demand and its implications for consumer behaviour.
Use indifference curve analysis to determine consumer equilibrium and marginal rate of substitution.
Analyse the impact of changes in consumer preferences, income and prices on demand and supply.
Evaluate the assumptions and limitations of consumer equilibrium.
board_exam_objectives: To examine the concept of consumer equilibrium and its conditions.
To apply indifference curve analysis to determine consumer equilibrium.
To evaluate the law of demand and its implications for consumer behaviour.
To analyse the impact of changes in consumer preferences, income and prices on demand and supply.
To identify the assumptions and limitations of consumer equilibrium.
